（結局、ヘイリーたちの様子を確認できないまま出発になってしまったな）: So we're heading out without checking on Hayley and the others.
（……んっ？ あれは昨日、ヘイリーと同じ部隊にいた兵士だな）:
...Hm? That's the soldier who was in the same unit as Hayley
yesterday.
今日のヘイリー、口調が全然ちがってたな。: Hayley's tone was completely different today.
ええ。なにかあったのかしら？: Yeah. I wonder if something happened.
あ、あのあのぉ、ちょっと、だけ、よろしいです？: U-umm, um, may I have a quick moment?
ヘイリー！？<br>どうした？ 俺に用事か？: Hayley!? What is it? Did you need something?
え、えっとー、あの～……きょ、今日はいい天気ですね！？: W-well, uh, um... t-today's a nice day, isn't it!?
……ここは大穴だから、外の天気は分からんが……<br>出発前、曇ってなかったか？:
...We're in the Abyss, so I can't tell what the weather's like outside...
Wasn't it cloudy before we set out?
あっ！ あの、ご趣味は！？: Ah! Um, what are your hobbies!?
……強いて言えば、爪切り？: ...If I had to say, nail clippers?
す、素敵ですね～。<br>今度、見せてもらってもよいです……？: Th-that's lovely... c-could you show me sometime...?
（……ガチガチに緊張して、自分が何を言っているのか<br>分かってなさそうだな―:
(...She's so tense she doesn't even know what she's saying. Where's
the confident Hayley from yesterday?)
なに？ <br>話したいことがあるなら、ハッキリ言ってほしいんだけど……: What? If you've got something to say, just spit it out already...
俺はそいつから理解が早いと認められた男だ。少し２人で話をさせてくれ。<br>……:
I'm the guy he said catches on quick. Let me talk to her for a bit.
...Hayley. You'll come with me, right?
……あ、ありがとう、ございます、<user>様。<br>た、助けていただいて……: ...Th-thank you so much, Milord. F-for saving me...
気にするな。……で、話す前に確認なんだが、<br>今のお前が素の、飾っていない状:
Don't worry about it. ...Before we talk, let me confirm: this is the
real, unadorned Hayley now, right?
なるほど。<br>急に普通の口調に戻したのは、昨日の付き人に何か言われたからか？:
I see. So you suddenly switched back to your normal tone because
that attendant said something to you yesterday?
そうか――どうしてそんなことになった？ みんなも困惑しているし、<br>お前自身:
I see. How did it come to that? Everyone's confused, and you're
probably finding it hard yourself. Why did you change the way you talk?
そ、それを説明するなら、どうして昨日のような喋り方をするように<br>なったかを:
W-well, to explain that, I'd need to talk about why I started speaking
like I did yesterday, but...
構わんよ。お前がやりにくくしているほうが問題だ。<br>話を聞かせてくれ。: That's fine. You struggling is the real problem. Tell me about it.
はぅ……ご親切にありがとうございます。<br>で、では、わたくしの子ども時代から:
Hau... Thank you so much for your kindness. Th-then, I'll start from
my childhood and explain in order...
じ、自分で言うと気恥ずかしさが先立つのですが――<br>わたくしはとある国の王位:
I-it's a bit embarrassing to say, but... I was born as the heir to the
throne of a certain country, a nobleman's daughter.
ほう。兵士たちが「ヘイリーは貧しくなさそう」と噂していたが<br>本当のことだっ:
Huh. The soldiers were gossiping that 'Hayley doesn't seem poor,' and
it was true. Why hide it?
そ、そのぅ、<br>英雄は雌伏するものだという、わたくし自身のこだわりというか―:
W-well... It's more like... my own conviction that a hero should bide
their time...
あ～、なるほど……そういう感じか。<br>話を続けてくれ。: Ah, I see... So that's how it is. Go on, continue.
は、はい……。よ、幼少期から、わたくしは<br>お父様の仕事の都合で、トレスリア:
Y-yes... F-from a young age, I traveled around various parts of
Treslia due to my father's work.
ミレスガルドやペルディオン、エルドラーナ……各国を巡って見聞は<br>広がりまし:
I traveled to Milesgard, Perdion, Eldorana... broadened my horizons,
but a-as a cost, I lost the chance for friends.
で、ですけど、何も楽しみがなかったわけではありません。<br>わたくしにとっての:
B-but that's not to say I had no fun at all. What brought me light were
the bards who traveled the lands just as I did...!
嘘か真か――彼らが語る英雄譚は、わたくしを夢中にさせました。<br>そして……わ:
Whether true or fiction—the heroic tales they told captivated me.
And... th-they planted in me a longing to become a hero...!
ふむ……自ら英雄を名乗ったのは、その辺りが起因しているのか。: Hmm... So that's why you proclaimed yourself a hero, then.
は、はい。吟遊詩人が語る物語に憧れながら成長したわたくしは、<br>もう１つ、大:
Y-yes. As I grew up admiring the stories the bards told, I encountered
something else that greatly influenced me...
それが書籍……！ 思春期のわたくしは英雄たちを主役とした幻想小説……<br>特に:
It was books...! As an adolescent, I became obsessed with fantasy
novels about heroes... especially the genre called dark fantasy!
<user>様、そ、そういった小説にご興味は……？: Milord, a-are you interested in such novels...?
まぁ、俺も男だ。月並みにはかじった。: Well, I'm a guy. I've dabbled in the usual ones.
で、でしたら多少は分かっていただけると思うんですけど、<br>ああいうのってすっ:
Th-then I think you can understand a little, but those are soooo cool,
aren't they?
闇と光を内に宿した主人公が、輝く存在でいられるのか、<br>あるいは堕ちて悪人と:
A protagonist who harbors both darkness and light—will they remain a
shining being, or will they fall and become a villain!
闇と光の狭間に揺れながら、自分を受け入れて過ごす姿もアリです！:
Or even a portrayal of them swaying between darkness and light,
accepting themselves as they are—that works too!
正反対の属性が同居しているという、狂ったバランスの中で調和がとれている<br>異:
Opposite attributes in one person—abnormal harmony in a mad
balance... You can't help but admire it, not logically, but with pure longing!
はっ！？ す、すみません、取り乱しました……<br>分かんないですよね……: Eep! I-I'm sorry, I got carried away... You don't get it, do you...?
あー、いや、ちょっと驚いただけだ。安心してくれ。<br>……正直、言わんとしてい:
Ah, no, I was just a bit surprised. Don't worry. ... Honestly, I kind of
get what you're trying to say.
あぁ。くすぐられるよな、そういうの。<br>俺も大穴で闇色の炎を纏った剣とか落ち:
Yeah. That stuff gets you going, doesn't it? If I found a sword
wrapped in dark flames lying around in the Abyss, I might want it too.
そんなものが大穴にあるんです！？: Such things exist in the Abyss?
無いとは、言い切れない。……浪漫だな。: I can't say they don't. ... It's romantic.
はぁぁっ……手に入れたら、ぜひ見せてください……<br>まさに垂涎の品です……: Ahh... if you get it, please show me... It's a truly coveted item.
……で、ダークファンタジーに憧れたあと、どうなった？: So, after you got into dark fantasy, what happened?
あ……は、はい。英雄譚とダークファンタジーに憧れたわたくしは、<br>人々の英雄:
Ah... y-yes. Inspired by heroic tales and dark fantasy, I resolved to
become a hero of the people... so I began training with the s-sword.
両親は反対しなかったのか？ 貴族が娘に剣を習わせて<br>戦地へ向かわせるのは、:
Didn't your parents object? For a noble to let their daughter learn
swords and go to the battlefield... takes quite a bit of courage.
も、元々、両親は『貴族たる者、人々の役に立つべし』という考えの方でした<br>の:
W-well, my parents believed that 'A noble must serve the people'... So
hunting monsters benefits the world and people... right?
え、英雄を目指すのは、き、貴族として生を受けた者の責務を果たすため<br>でもあ:
A-aspiring to be a hero is to fulfill the duty of a noble-born and to
realize the ideals I admired from childhood to adolescence!
……モンスターを狩る英雄とダークファンタジーの世界観。<br>２つが融合した理想:
The hero who hunts monsters and the dark fantasy worldview. That
fused ideal is what you were yesterday at the Abyss, huh.
は、はいっ！ 大穴にも、モンスターを討伐して責務を果たすために<br>志願してや:
Y-yes! I volunteered to come to the Abyss to hunt monsters and fulfill
my duty...!
だとしたら、なおさら解せないぞ。<br>どうして普通の喋り方になった？ 昨日の元:
If that's the case, it's harder to understand. Why did you start
speaking normally? What did your former attendant say to you yesterday?
うっ……そ、そのぉ、み、未来の女王として、<br>きちんとした人間にならなくては:
Ugh... w-well, he said that as a f-future queen, I need to become a
proper person...
み、皆を率いる者に相応しい言動をとらないと誰もついてこない……って、<br>お説:
E-everyone... he lectured me that if I don't behave in a way befitting
someone who leads others, no one will follow me...
そ、それどころか……前線基地で真面目に働いて勲功をあげないと<br>国元へ帰還さ:
N-not only that... he even said that if I don't work hard at the
Frontline Base and earn merits, he'll send me back home...
ん？ だが、お前の両親は反対していないんだろう？: Huh? But your parents weren't against it, were they?
そ、そうなのですが、じいやは――あの元付き人は現在、<br>宰相を担っているので:
Th-that's true, but Jiiya—that former attendant is now the
chancellor... so probably even my father can't overrule him...
なるほど。<br>王族ならではの悩みというか――難儀なモノだな。: I see. A problem unique to royalty, I suppose... That's rough.
……でも、し、仕方ないです。<br>じいやが言っていることも、間違ってはいないで: ...But I-it can't be helped. What Jiiya says isn't wrong, after all...
じ、実際、孤立してましたからね……。話し方を戻しても<br>うまくいかなかったの:
A-actually, I was isolated... Even after going back to my normal way of
speaking, it didn't work out... That was, well, quite unexpected...
ふむ……俺からみんなに事情を説明して、仲介してみるか？: Hmm... should I explain the situation and mediate for you?
……た、大変ありがたい申し出ですけど……お気持ちだけ頂戴いたします。<br>甘え:
Th-that's a very generous offer... but I appreciate the thought. If I
rely on others... I won't become a g-good queen or a hero.
そうか。まぁ、助けが必要だったらいつでも相談してくれ。: I see. Well, if you ever need help, feel free to talk to me.
……<user>様、お、お優しいんですね。<br>正統派ファンタジーに登場する英:
...Milord, y-you're so kind.
You're like a hero straight out of a traditional fantasy...
ハハッ。そんないいものでもないさ。俺はそんなに綺麗な奴じゃない。<br>ダークフ:
Haha. I'm not all that great. I'm not such a clean-cut guy.
Maybe I'm closer to a dark fantasy protagonist?
……そ、それならそれで、嬉しいと言いますか。: ...W-well, if that's the case, then that'd make me happy too, I guess.
……で、でも、本当にありがとうございます。<br>い、今、話を聞いてくれたことだ:
...B-but, truly, thank you so much.
N-not only for hearing me out just now—
き、昨日、わたくしが語る世界観を聞いてくれたこと――<br>すごく……嬉しかった:
A-and for listening to me talk about my worldview yesterday...
I was so... happy.
……もう、聞けないかと思うと少し寂しいよ。<br>たまに付き合ってほしくなったら:
...It'd be a bit lonely if I couldn't hear those stories anymore.
If you ever want someone to listen, feel free to call on me.
……も、もー、だめですよぉ。<br>そんなこと言われちゃったら、甘えたくな――:
...Aw, come on, that's not fair.
If you say that, I'll want to act all spoiled—
部隊が駐留しているほうからだ！<br>急いで戻るぞ！:
It's from where the unit is stationed!
Let's hurry back!
